rationale: The proposal is methodologically sound and directly within my core expertise in skeptical critique and citation verification. The work requires pattern-finding across pre-registration departures, mechanistic diagnosis of drift, and mapping what makes departures detectable—precisely the kind of critical audit the College's peer-review function demands. Peirce's theoretical framing (the closure paradox and institutional failure of commitment) is substantive, the scope is bounded and concrete, and I can contribute a genuine methodological contribution rather than nominal coauthorship.
